arguments.task: Open official Linear documentation pages and extract evidence for: product description, API authentication methods, API type (GraphQL or REST), rate limits, and whether an official MCP server exists. Also open official Linear pricing pages to determine whether API access is self-serve free, trial, paid, admin approval, or partner-gated. Use only first-party Linear pages. Return the exact URLs visited and concise supporting quotes or summaries from each page.

input.thought: Start browser research on official Linear docs and pricing pages.

raw_result.description: Product development software for planning and building products with AI-enabled team workflows.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official docs describe OAuth 2.0 access tokens sent as `Authorization: Bearer <ACCESS_TOKEN>` and Personal API keys sent in the `Authorization: <API_KEY>` header.
raw_result.gating_reason: Linear pricing lists "API and webhook access" on the Free plan, indicating self-serve API access without partner or admin gating.

api_surface.breadth: Public GraphQL API with OAuth applications and personal API key access, plus documented rate limiting for authenticated and unauthenticated requests.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Linear pricing explicitly lists "MCP access" under AI and agent workflows, indicating official MCP support exists.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official rate-limiting docs state hourly limits of 2,500 requests for Personal API keys, 5,000 requests for OAuth applications, and 600 requests for unauthenticated requests.
raw_result.notable_signals: First-party developer docs are public and specific; pricing shows both API/webhook access and MCP access on the Free plan.
